purpose: Stress-test how superficially strong scoring cases can remain readable without creating new material escalation or genuine S2-entry pressure.

entries.stress_pattern: symbolic maximum looks like fresh review pressure
entries.primary_misreading: A symbolic maximum could be misread as a live scoring emergency rather than as an already isolated worldview signal.
entries.s2_entry_relation: Looks theoretically hot on the surface but does not demonstrate a missing interpretive axis that would justify S2 entry work.

entries.stress_pattern: local strength gets overstated as registry-wide exception
entries.primary_misreading: Strong usefulness inside one domain can be mistaken for registry-wide exceptional value.
entries.s2_entry_relation: Shows domain fit and bounded usefulness, not a failure of current S1 plus human review.

entries.stress_pattern: schema or genealogy language gets misread as true S2 proximity
entries.primary_misreading: A future-facing schema proposal can sound like evidence that a second scoring layer is already needed.
entries.s2_entry_relation: Future design interest alone does not establish genuine S2-entry relevance.

entries.stress_pattern: formal compactness or gate language overstates evidential hardness
entries.primary_misreading: A compact gate framework can be misread as stronger evidence than the heuristic material actually supports.
entries.s2_entry_relation: Compact gate language does not by itself reveal a missing S2 semantics problem.
